items.title: The Download: soccer’s data renaissance and China’s big nuclear plans
items.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. items.title: Google DeepMind is worried about what happens when millions of agents start to interact
items.summary: Google DeepMind is funding research into the potential dangers of situations where millions of different AI agents interact with each other online. According to Rohin Shah, who directs the company’s AGI safety and alignment research, the mass-market arrival of agents that can carry out tasks without human oversight and follow instructions given to them by other…

items.title: OpenAI to acquire Ona
items.summary: OpenAI plans to acquire Ona to expand Codex with secure, persistent cloud environments, enabling long-running AI agents across enterprise workflows.

items.title: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
items.summary: OpenAI supports the EU Code of Practice on AI content transparency, advancing provenance standards and tools to help people understand AI-generated content.

items.title: BBVA puts AI at the core of banking with OpenAI
items.summary: Learn how BBVA scaled ChatGPT Enterprise to 100,000 employees and partnered with OpenAI to accelerate AI-powered banking transformation worldwide.

items.title: Access OpenAI models and Codex through your Oracle cloud commitment
items.summary: Access OpenAI models and Codex through Oracle Cloud, using existing commitments to build and deploy AI with enterprise security and governance.

items.title: Amazon’s Echo Hub gets a customizable new look and Ring’s AI features
items.summary: Amazon's rolling out a free software update for Echo Hub devices that gives the home screen a much-needed update to the interface it launched with in 2024. It had already added Alex Plus AI support, but the new interface has a cleaner, fully customizable layout that fits more smart home info and controls on the […]

items.title: OpenAI to acquire Ona to support its AI coding assistant, Codex
items.summary: Ona's technology will allow OpenAI's coding assistant, Codex, to take on longer-running tasks, OpenAI said.

items.title: SpaceX raising $75 billion in record-setting IPO as Nasdaq debut awaits
items.summary: SpaceX is selling 555.6 shares at $135 a piece, raising $75 billion in the largest IPO on record.

items.title: My elderly mother and I own a home together. Will Medicaid force its sale?
items.summary: “My sister thinks Medicaid will take the house my mom and I own together.”

items.title: From startup to $1.8 trillion: The investors who took a chance on SpaceX now reap the rewards
items.summary: With SpaceX seeking an IPO valuation of nearly $1.8 trillion, early bets are poised to generate some of the biggest paper gains in venture capital history.

items.title: Warren questions SpaceX IPO oversight in new letter to stock indexes
items.summary: Sen. Elizabeth Warren seeking answers on changes to index provider waiting periods, protections for retail investors, in a letter obtained first by CNBC.

items.title: Trump picks former SEC Chairman Jay Clayton as national intelligence director
items.summary: Trump's announcement came after House Democrats' objections to his pick of Bill Pulte as acting chief ensured that a key national security tool expires Friday.

items.title: SpaceX cuts retail IPO allocation to low 20% range, source says
items.summary: The Elon Musk-led company plans to direct a percentage in the low 20s of the offering to retail buyers, according to a person familiar with the matter.

items.title: We asked AI to predict the 2026 World Cup winner. It picked a team that’s never won.
